verbatim_text: So they try to clone or distill this auxiliary data directly towards improving exploration. That does improve training rewards. But in the end, what you learn are algorithms that don't generalize very broadly. So what we try to do is slightly different. We look at this auxiliary data and we try to improve or bootstrap online exploration with this auxiliary data. Previously, we were only giving this hard problem as input to the LLM. Now we append to it this offline auxiliary data. And with the right choice of this data, we looked at a few options previously. With the right choice of this data, you can now condition on this new state and generate completions that are online from the algorithm. And many of these online completions can now fetch correct rewards. So the key insight here is that conditioning on the right kind of offline auxiliary data can simplify online search and improve future success. Now, this is a very simple but powerful way that can help us define our surrogate sample that we're looking for. Remember that the job of the sampler is to explore widely and improve exploration. And here we have a way where you can condition on the right offline auxiliary data in addition to the problem.
